content.text:
This rotated view confirms the bonding clearly. Atom **O:13** is connected only to **N:11** via a single bond. This is part of a nitro group (–NO₂) where N:11 is bonded to both O:12 and O:13.

## Results

**Atom of interest:** O:13 (Oxygen, index 13)

**Neighbour atoms (covalently bonded):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 11 | N (Nitrogen) |

**geomconf_index:** **5493**

The oxygen atom O:13 has **one** covalent neighbour: **N:11**. This is consistent with the nitro group (–NO₂) structure, where each oxygen is bonded to the central nitrogen atom.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 13, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
